name: MG.Alert

values.name: render
values.comment: Because we generally expect alerts to be displayed conditionally, this function takes a `Maybe Alert`. If the value is `Nothing`, then no alert is rendered.

name: MG.Attributes

values.name: onEnter
values.comment: Add an onEnter event to any element in Elm-UI. Generally intended for use on input fields.

values.name: testIdentifier
values.comment: Add a data-test attribute to any element in Elm-UI. For use in e2e testing.

values.name: transition
values.comment: Add standard transition to any element in Elm-UI. For use on elements where styles will change depending on some state change.

aliases.name: Gradient
aliases.comment:
Define your gradient.
    ID must be globally unique in your HTML.
    Direction is degrees clockwise from 12 o'clock.
aliases.type: { id : String.String, startColour : Element.Color, endColour : Element.Color, direction : Basics.Float }

name: MG.Input

values.name: disabledEmail
values.comment: An element that looks like an input field, but is disabled and contains a button to change the value. For use when a user has already entered their email address. `value` is a (Char, String) to ensure that this element can not be created with an empty value.
values.type: { value : ( Char.Char, String.String ), enableMsg : msg, viewport : MG.Viewport.Viewport, isFocused : Basics.Bool } -> Element.Element msg

values.name: enabledEmail
values.comment: Common email field with animated placeholder
values.type: { onChange : String.String -> msg, onEnter : msg, value : String.String, errorStr : Maybe.Maybe String.String, onSubmit : Maybe.Maybe msg, isFocused : Basics.Bool, viewport : MG.Viewport.Viewport } -> Element.Element msg

values.name: password
values.comment: Common email field with animated placeholder and show/hide button.
values.type: { onChange : String.String -> msg, value : String.String, showValue : Basics.Bool, togglePasswordVisibility : Basics.Bool -> msg, viewport : MG.Viewport.Viewport, onSubmit : Maybe.Maybe msg, isFocused : Basics.Bool } -> Element.Element msg

name: MG.Typography
MG.Viewport
comment:
# Viewport

How layouts work horizontally.

| Margin | Column | Gutter | Column | Gutter | ... | Gutter | Column | Margin |

There are two margins, on the extreme left and the extreme right.

There are gutters separating each column, so there is one fewer gutter
than there are columns.

Default360 has 6 columns (and 5 gutters). All other sizes have 12 columns
(and 11 gutters).

The margin widths and gutter widths are hard coded per viewport size.
The remaining width is divided equally between each column.

A component will span n columns (and therefore also the n-1 gutters between).


# Managing Viewport differences

@docs Viewport, Width, PerBreakpoint, perBreakpoint


# Classifying Viewport

@docs classify, classifyDefault360

unions.name: Width
unions.comment: Default is 360px wide, no reason to guarantee anything will work below this.

aliases.name: PerBreakpoint
aliases.comment: Helper to provide a different `a` for each viewport size.
aliases.type: { default360 : a, atLeast768Wide : a, atLeast1024Wide : a, atLeast1440Wide : a }

aliases.name: Viewport
aliases.type: { size : MG.Viewport.Width, height : Basics.Int, width : Basics.Int, marginWidth : Basics.Int, gutterWidth : Basics.Int, spanningColumnsWidth : Basics.Int -> Basics.Int, contentWidth : Basics.Int, columnCount : Basics.Int }

values.name: classifyDefault360
values.comment: Forces a viewport that always returns Default360 for its size.

values.name: perBreakpoint
values.comment: Helper to retrieve a different `a` for each viewport size.
